action: Click the Windows Start menu button to open the Start menu.
fallback_action: Perform a Google search using Internet Explorer

recipe.steps:
is the first action. The task is to click the Windows Start menu button to open the Start menu. On the screen, the Start menu button is typically represented by the Windows logo. The detected bounding boxes include Box ID 189 labeled 'Start', which matches the Windows Start menu button. I will click this button to proceed.
Action Description: Click the Windows Start menu button to open the Start menu.

previous action: The previous action was to click the Windows Start menu button (Box ID 189) to open the Start menu. On the current screen, the Start menu is now open, as indicated by the presence of the Start menu UI and various application icons and options. This confirms the previous action succeeded. The next step is to search for Notepad to open it, as the task involves opening Notepad and typing 'hello'. I will type 'Notepad' to search for the application.
Action Description: Type 'Notepad' in the Start menu search bar to search for the Notepad application.

previous action: The previous action was to type 'Notepad' in the Start menu search bar. On the current screen, I can see the Notepad application icon (Box ID 129) in the Start menu, which confirms that the search was successful. The next step is to open Notepad by clicking on its icon.
Action Description: Click the Notepad application icon to open Notepad.

previous action: The previous action was to click the Notepad application icon in the Start menu. On the current screen, I can see the Notepad window is now open, as indicated by the Notepad icon (Box ID 142) and the typical Notepad UI layout. The next step is to type 'hello' in the Notepad text area. Since Notepad's main text area does not have a bounding box, I will use the visual segmentation approach and type directly, assuming the focus is in the text area after opening Notepad.
Action Description: Type 'hello' in the Notepad text area.
